metadata.title:
THE TRIAL OF S. T.


CRANE

pages.raw_text:
Samuel T. Crane, I. w. w. attor-
ney, was placed on trial this morn
ing in Judge ]. D. Hinkle's depart
ment of the superior court, the case
being an appeal from a conviction
for disorderly conduct secured in
the police court


The morning session was devoted
to the selection of q jury. Crane APP
pearing in his own behalf. and As
sistant Corporation >Counsels Sar-'
gent and Blair for the city. The
following were accepted as Jurors:
c. c. Fishback, VIM. Elmquist, VIM.
Bennett, ]. S. Inkster, John Woe
hill. c. e. Fay, John A. Bauer, A. B.
Bartlett, G. e. Lawrence, A. e.
Beaughan, A. c. Hembling and F.
II. Berry.


When Attorney Crane asked
those of the venire to stand up who
were preJudiced against the l. w.
W., three of the twelve rose to
their. feet. In examining the tales
men, Crane questioned them along
the line of their feeling towards
the Industrial Workers, their. .atti-,
tude on free speech, whether they
inherited wealth or accumulated it
as a worker. Such questions as,
you are not in sympathy with
this muzzling of free speech, are
you?" or do you think it good
policy to prosecute every little
thing?" were objected to, of course,
but they show the nature of the ex-
animation.


Crane was arrested for address
ing crowd of I. w. WIS from his
office window in the second story
of the Columbia building. y
